examples.title: Check if Relation is Reflexive
examples.explanation: Checks if every element in set A is related to itself. Must contain all n diagonal pairs (a,a).

examples.title: Check if Relation is Symmetric
examples.explanation: For every ordered pair, its reverse must also be in the relation. Checks pairs in both directions.

examples.title: Check if Relation is Transitive
examples.explanation: If (a,b) and (b,c) exist, then (a,c) must also exist. Tests all possible chains of length 2.

examples.title: Compute Composition R ∘ S
examples.explanation: R ∘ S = {(a,c) : ∃b such that (a,b)∈S and (b,c)∈R}. Applies S first, then R.

examples.title: Find Inverse Relation
examples.explanation: R⁻¹ swaps each ordered pair. If (a,b)∈R, then (b,a)∈R⁻¹. Always symmetric to original.

examples.title: Verify Equivalence Relation
examples.code:
FUNCTION IsEquivalenceRelation(R, A)
 IF NOT IsReflexive(R, A) THEN
 RETURN FALSE
 IF NOT IsSymmetric(R) THEN
 RETURN FALSE
 IF NOT IsTransitive(R) THEN
 RETURN FALSE
 RETURN TRUE
END FUNCTION

// Returns TRUE only if reflexive, symmetric, AND transitive
examples.explanation: Combines three property checks. An equivalence relation partitions the set into disjoint equivalence classes.
